sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 10.74 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.10)

statement.latex: Suppose that a group$G$contains an element$a$of prime order such that its centralizer$C_G(a)$is finite and all subgroups$< a,\, a^g >$,$g G$, are finite and almost all of them are soluble. Is$G$locally finite? This problem is closely connected with 6.56. The question was solved in the positive for a number of very important partial cases by the author (Abstracts on Group Theory of the Mal'cev Int....

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
